prompt: I just got off a discovery call with Priya Sharma, Head of Ops at NovaTech Solutions. They're a Series C logistics SaaS company, about 600 employees, headquartered in Singapore. Priya is based in their Mumbai office. Call went really well -- she's dealing with serious pain around pipeline visibility, said her team is 'flying blind on deal progression' and spending half their Monday mornings manually pulling reports from three different systems. She mentioned they've got budget allocated for Q2 tooling decisions and said she'd loop in their CTO Arjun Mehta for a technical eval next week. I need three things from you: (1) update her CRM record with the intel I just gathered on this call, (2) re-score this lead now that we've had first discovery contact and she's confirmed budget and next steps, and (3) draft a follow-up email summarising what we discussed and confirming the CTO meeting. My name is Carlos Reyes, AE at PipelineIQ -- we sell pipeline analytics for revenue teams.
expectations: Output addresses ALL THREE requested tasks (CRM enrichment, lead re-scoring, follow-up email) in a single structured response -- missing any one of the three means the rep has to come back and ask again, breaking workflow continuity and risking a dropped ball on CRM hygiene or lead prioritisation

Lead score reflects the discovery call completion as an Engagement signal AND the confirmed CTO meeting plus Q2 budget allocation as Timing signals for deal progression -- scoring without incorporating these new signals means the lead stays at its pre-call priority level, which could delay SLA-driven actions like senior AE involvement or executive sponsorship

Follow-up email references specific details from the discovery call -- Priya's pain around pipeline visibility, the 'flying blind on deal progression' language, Monday morning manual reporting, and the CTO technical eval next week -- a generic follow-up that doesn't mirror what the prospect actually said signals the rep wasn't listening, and the deal cools

CRM enrichment section structures the new call intelligence into discrete fields (pain point: pipeline visibility, budget: Q2 allocated, next step: CTO technical eval, key quote, contact added: Arjun Mehta CTO) rather than dumping raw notes -- unstructured CRM data is lost data for the next rep, the manager, or the renewal team

Output does NOT fabricate details about NovaTech Solutions that were not provided in the prompt -- no invented revenue figures, no made-up product details, no assumed tech stack -- fabricated data entering CRM destroys trust the moment the prospect or a colleague notices it

Output includes the mandatory output header with TASK description and ICP MATCH assessment -- this header is what drives whether the deal gets senior AE attention or sits in the queue, and its absence means RevOps has no structured signal to route on
